title: Intensely Smoky Jamaican Sesame Seed Flatbread
description: This smoky flatbread features a long-fermentation dough and is perfect for a quick Jamaican-inspired snack, packed with sesame seeds and a dash of spice, served in a convenient bento box.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, yeast, salt, smoked paprika, sugar, and jerk seasoning.
Gradually add water to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 2 hours.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Punch down the fermented dough and divide it into 4 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Brush the dough circles with a little water and sprinkle sesame seeds evenly over the surface.
Cook the flatbreads in the preheated skillet for 2 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the smoky sesame seed flatbreads warm in a bento box, perfect for a quick snack or lunch on the go.
